top_signals.title: Broadcom stock plunges 12% on weak software sales, unchanged AI chip forecast for the year
top_signals.summary: Broadcom reported fiscal second-quarter results on Wednesday and missed estimates for revenue.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China’s Chery poised to strike deal with Nissan to build cars at Sunderland plant
top_signals.summary: Chery is on the cusp of building its first manufacturing presence in the UK in the latest sign of Chinese carmakers gaining a major foothold in Britain’s electric vehicle market. The Omoda and Jaecoo owner, which is the third largest carmaker in China, has unveiled a non-binding memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Nissan to explore [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Energy Costs Are Catching Up With Big Tech
top_signals.summary: Artificial intelligence is all the rage these days, and so is its energy consumption. From a game-changing technology that would usher in a new era, AI has recently turned into a problem for many—a problem that makes electricity more expensive while, apparently, not really living up to the promise of its developers. Big Tech is still pledging tens of billions in AI spend. Now, it may have to spend a bigger portion of those billions on securing its own energy, and on proving its worth the money that AI users would have to spend on the technology.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
